title: Report finds Syracuse lead paint ordinance may not be preventing poisoning cases in kids
summary: A report by Families for Lead Freedom Now and Earthjustice finds that Syracuse's 2022 lead paint ordinance, despite generating 7,000+ citations, is not reducing childhood lead poisoning cases. The analysis reveals enforcement gaps including repeated citations on same properties without follow-up and over half of rental properties unregistered. Code Enforcement and Health Department officials maintain the ordinance is still in early stages and expect longer-term data to demonstrate impact.

political_signal.description: External advocacy organization and legal partner conducting independent audit of ordinance effectiveness; findings contradict city enforcement claims regarding policy impact on public health outcomes

processing_notes: Article from June 2025 - future date suggests hypothetical scenario
Mechanical extraction error: Law Department not mentioned in article; correction: Code Enforcement is primary department
Report title 'Falling through the Cracks' should be tracked as key deliverable
